ucs.title: [LLM] Storm-1849 (UAT4356 / ArcaneDoor) — Cisco ASA WebVPN auth anomaly chained to internal AD reconnaissance
ucs.description: Hunts the ArcaneDoor entry pattern where Storm-1849 exploits Cisco ASA edge devices (CVE-2024-20353 / CVE-2024-20359) and reuses valid VPN/admin accounts (Line Dancer in-memory implant) to authenticate inward, then immediately performs LDAP/SMB recon against domain controllers from the VPN-assigned subnet. The 'VPN logon from low-prevalence ASN → DC enumeration within minutes' chain is the actor's signature pivot, not generic VPN abuse.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as first_seen max(_time) as last_seen values(Authentication.src) as vpn_src values(Authentication.app) as auth_app from datamodel=Authentication where Authentication.app IN ("cisco:asa","cisco:anyconnect","ciscoasa","cisco_vpn") Authentication.action=success by Authentication.user Authentication.src_ip _time span=1h | `drop_dm_object_name(Authentication)` | iplocation src_ip | search country!="United States" OR Country="" | join type=inner user [ | tstats summariesonly=true count as recon_hits values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as ports values(All_Traffic.dest) as dest_dcs from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where (All_Traffic.dest_port IN (88,389,445,636,3268) AND All_Traffic.dest_category="domain_controller") by All_Traffic.user All_Traffic.src _time span=1h | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)` | rename user as user ] | where recon_hits > 25 AND (last_seen - first_seen) < 600 | table _time user src_ip country auth_app dest_dcs ports recon_hits
ucs.kql: let suspectVpnLogons = AADSignInEventsBeta | where Timestamp > ago(7d) | where AppDisplayName has_any ("Cisco AnyConnect","AnyConnect","ASA") or ResourceDisplayName has "VPN" | where ErrorCode == 0 | extend vpnAsn = tostring(parse_json(NetworkLocationDetails)[0].networkType) | where Country !in ("US","GB","CA") or isempty(Country) | project vpnTime=Timestamp, AccountObjectId, AccountUpn, IPAddress, Country, vpnAsn; let dcRecon = IdentityDirectoryEvents | where Timestamp > ago(7d) | where ActionType in ("LDAP search","SAMR query","DCSync","Account enumeration") | summarize reconCount=count(), dcTargets=make_set(TargetDeviceName), actions=make_set(ActionType) by AccountObjectId, bin(Timestamp, 10m) | where reconCount > 20; suspectVpnLogons | join kind=inner dcRecon on AccountObjectId | where Timestamp between (vpnTime .. vpnTime + 15m) | project vpnTime, Timestamp, AccountUpn, IPAddress, Country, vpnAsn, reconCount, dcTargets, actions
ucs.rationale: Generic VPN-anomaly rules fire constantly on traveling users. This UC narrows to the exact Storm-1849 ArcaneDoor sequence Cisco Talos and Microsoft documented: edge-device exploitation yielding a valid VPN session that, within minutes, performs unusual LDAP/SAMR enumeration of DCs — Line Dancer's hallmark internal pivot. Time-bounding the join (<10–15m) and requiring high-volume DC port hits filte

ucs.title: [LLM] Storm-1849 Line Runner persistence — beaconing from Cisco ASA management plane to rare destination
ucs.description: Detects the Line Runner persistence implant Storm-1849 plants on Cisco ASA appliances by hunting low-prevalence outbound HTTPS/SSH from ASA management IPs to internet destinations the device has never previously contacted, paired with disabled syslog forwarding (T1562.006) — the actor's documented anti-forensics step before C2 callback.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.src_category="network_appliance_mgmt" OR All_Traffic.src IN (<ASA_MGMT_CIDRS>) All_Traffic.dest_port IN (443,22,8443) NOT All_Traffic.dest_category="internal" by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.dest All_Traffic.dest_port All_Traffic.bytes_out _time span=1h | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)` | eventstats dc(dest) as historical_dest_count by src | stats count sum(bytes_out) as total_bytes values(dest_port) as ports min(_time) as first_seen max(_time) as last_seen by src dest historical_dest_count | where count < 50 AND total_bytes < 5000000 AND historical_dest_count < 10 | join type=left src [ search index=cisco sourcetype=cisco:asa ("logging disable" OR "no logging host" OR "%ASA-7-111009") | stats count as logging_changes values(_time) as logging_change_time by host | rename host as src ] | where isnotnull(logging_changes) | iplocation dest | table first_seen src dest dest_country ports total_bytes count logging_changes logging_change_time
ucs.kql: let asaMgmtIps = dynamic(["10.10.250.0/24","10.20.250.0/24"]); let rareEgress = DeviceNetworkEvents | where Timestamp > ago(14d) | where ipv4_is_in_any_range(LocalIP, asaMgmtIps) or RemoteIPType == "Public" and DeviceName has_any ("asa","firewall","edge") | where RemotePort in (443, 22, 8443) | where ActionType == "ConnectionSuccess" | summarize beacons=count(), totalBytes=sum(toint(coalesce(tostring(parse_json(AdditionalFields).bytes_out),"0"))), firstSeen=min(Timestamp), lastSeen=max(Timestamp) by LocalIP, RemoteIP, RemotePort | extend durationMin = (lastSeen - firstSeen) / 1m | where beacons between (5 .. 200) and durationMin > 60 and totalBytes < 5000000; let prevalentDests = DeviceNetworkEvents | where Timestamp between (ago(60d) .. ago(14d)) | where RemoteIPType == "Public" | summarize seenCount=count() by RemoteIP | where seenCount > 50; rareEgress | join kind=leftanti prevalentDests on RemoteIP | extend RemoteASN = tostring(parse_json(AdditionalFields).RemoteASN) | project firstSeen, lastSeen, LocalIP, RemoteIP, RemoteASN, RemotePort, beacons, totalBytes, durationMin
ucs.rationale: Storm-1849's Line Runner / Line Dancer toolset specifically hides on ASA appliances and the actor was observed disabling logging immediately before establishing C2 — Cisco Talos called this out as a unique anti-forensic tell. Combining 'first-time-seen egress from mgmt IP' with 'preceding logging-disable command' is highly specific to ArcaneDoor and won't fire on routine vendor update beacons or a
